answer:
**'Ishq par zor nahin'** refers to the famous *maqtā* (signature closing couplet) from Mirza Ghalib's ghazal number 157 in his *Diwan-e-Ghalib*, titled "Nukta-cheen hai gham-e-dil uss ko sunaye na bane."[^1]

In transliteration (Romanized Urdu):  
**Ishq par zor nahin hai yeh woh aatish Ghalib**  
**Ke lagaye na lage aur bujhaye na bane**[^2]

In English translation:  
**On love, O Ghalib, you can have no force or sway;**  
**It is a fire you can’t light, or allay—it cannot be.**[^3]

This couplet likens love (*ishq*) to an uncontrollable fire: it cannot be ignited at will nor extinguished once kindled. It encapsulates Ghalib's philosophy on the involuntary, sovereign nature of passionate love, a recurring theme in his poetry.[^4]

The full ghazal (9 couplets) explores frustration in expressing grief, unrequited longing, rivalry, and the inescapability of fate and desire, building to this poignant conclusion.[^5]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, chapter 157, [translation](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/157-nukta-cheen-hai-gham-e-dil-uss-ko-sunaye-na-bane/translation/).

[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, chapter 157, paragraph [p-f9837a](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/157-nukta-cheen-hai-gham-e-dil-uss-ko-sunaye-na-bane/transliteration/#p-f9837a) (closing lines).

[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, chapter 157, paragraph [p-fb741d](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/157-nukta-cheen-hai-gham-e-dil-uss-ko-sunaye-na-bane/translation/#p-fb741d).

[^4]: Ibid. (maqtā).

[^5]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, chapter 157, [full passage](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/157-nukta-cheen-hai-gham-e-dil-uss-ko-sunaye-na-bane/translation/#p-b56c2d) (paragraphs p-b56c2d to p-fb741d).
